hero.title: Frequently Asked Questions
hero.subtitle: Everything you need to know about No.JS.

q1.question: What is No.JS?
q1.answer: An HTML-first reactive framework. State, data fetching, routing, i18n, animations: all declared through HTML attributes. Validation and drag-and-drop are now available via NoJS Elements. Zero dependencies, no build step.

q2.question: How do I get started?
q2.answer:
Add one script tag:

<script src="https://cdn.no-js.dev/"></script>

No build step, no package manager. Start writing attributes.

q3.question: Do I need to know JavaScript?
q3.answer:
Basic knowledge helps for writing expressions in attributes, but you won't write JavaScript files. Everything is declarative: state, bind, on:click, each.

For advanced use cases like custom filters or directives, you'll write some JS. The goal is to reduce, not eliminate.

q4.question: Can I use it with existing projects?
q4.answer: Yes. Add the script tag to any HTML page. No.JS only processes elements with its attributes, so it won't interfere with existing code or other libraries. Progressive adoption by design.

q5.question: How does reactivity work?
q5.answer: JavaScript Proxy objects. When you declare state, the framework wraps your data in a Proxy. On every property change, the set trap notifies registered watchers, which update the DOM directly. No virtual DOM, no diffing.

q6.question: What about computed state and async data?
q6.answer:
Computed: computed + expr recalculates automatically when dependencies change.

Async: get, post, put, patch, delete fetch data into the reactive context. Use into to pipe results to a global store.

Batching: multiple changes in the same cycle are deduped. Watchers fire once.

q7.question: What features are included?
q7.answer:
Everything ships in one file:

• Reactive state and computed properties
• SPA routing with lazy loading
• Declarative HTTP (GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE)
• i18n with external locale files
• Form validation (Now in NoJS Elements)
• Animations and transitions
• Drag and drop (Now in NoJS Elements)
• 32 built-in filters

q8.question: What's the right use case?
q8.answer:
Landing pages, dashboards, internal tools, prototypes, anything where you need reactivity without the ceremony of a full framework.

If business logic stays in the backend where it belongs, the frontend never needs to be complex enough to justify a heavy framework.

q9.question: No.JS vs Alpine.js?
q9.answer:
Alpine is reactive and lightweight, but lacks declarative HTTP, SPA routing, i18n, and built-in filters.

No.JS covers the full lifecycle: fetch, bind, loop, route, animate, all through HTML attributes. Form validation and drag-and-drop are available via NoJS Elements.

q10.question: No.JS vs HTMX?
q10.answer:
HTMX excels at server-driven HTML. It assumes a backend that returns HTML fragments.

No.JS works with any API (JSON endpoints, REST, public APIs) without requiring a server to template responses. Different philosophy: HTMX extends the server, No.JS extends the browser.

q11.question: No.JS vs React or Vue?
q11.answer:
React and Vue require a build step, a virtual DOM, and JavaScript files. No.JS requires none of that.

HTML files vs compiled bundles. Deployment means uploading HTML, not configuring a pipeline.

q12.question: This reminds me of AngularJS. Is it similar?
q12.answer:
Same intuition: bring web development back to HTML. Different execution: zero dependencies, no complexity spiral.

No.JS stays intentionally small and focused on the use cases where a full framework is overkill.

q13.question: How does No.JS handle security?
q13.answer:
Three layers of protection:

• HTML sanitization: strips <script> tags, event handlers, and javascript: protocols
• Scope isolation: each component's context is isolated via Proxy
• CSP-safe by default: expressions are evaluated by a custom parser — no eval() or Function() constructor is used

q14.question: How big is the bundle?
q14.answer: It includes reactivity, routing, data fetching, i18n, animations, and 32 filters. Drag-and-drop and form validation are now in NoJS Elements. Zero dependencies.

q15.question: Do I need a build step?
q15.answer: No. One script tag, zero configuration. No compilation, no transpilation, no bundling. Deployment is uploading HTML files.

q16.question: Is it production-ready?
q16.answer: Yes. 1,350+ unit tests, tested across modern browsers. This documentation site is built entirely with No.JS.
